01_initial_request.input_data: Individual cells (nano- to femto-liter fluidic samples)
01_initial_request.user_intent: To comprehensively characterize proteins at the single-cell level using microfluidics to complement genetic measurements and elucidate translational processes
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Enhanced proteome depth and sensitivity to detect proteins ranging from high abundance to low copy numbers with minimal sample loss

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Single-cell proteomics (SCP) reveals phenotypic heterogeneity but requires isolation of individual cells prior to processing. [Gap] Conventional ensemble measurements average differences among cells, and standard isolation methods incur sample loss during transfer. [Decision] To achieve minimal sample loss and precise handling, chose to use SciProChip (Cell-capture module) to perform size-based capturing of mammalian cells on-chip, expecting to obtain isolated single cells within seconds.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Size-based capturing of mammalian cells within a few seconds with real time microscopy monitoring.

references.citation: S. T. Gebreyesus et al., Nat. Commun., 2022, 13, 37 (Ref 10)
references.relevance: Describes the development and cell-capture functionality of the SciProChip device.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Isolated cells require lysis to release intracellular proteins for downstream analysis. [Gap] Off-chip lysis involves sample transfer which leads to significant sample loss at the single-cell level. [Decision] To achieve streamlined workflow with minimal loss, chose to use SciProChip (Lysis chamber) to perform on-chip cell lysis within the capture chamber, expecting to obtain released cell contents without transfer.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Cell lysis triggered in microchambers, avoiding sample losses due to sample transfer.

references.citation: S. T. Gebreyesus et al., Nat. Commun., 2022, 13, 37 (Ref 10)
references.relevance: Details the integrated cell-capture and lysis chamber design.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Proteins must be digested into peptides for mass spectrometry analysis. [Gap] Traditional digestion in microvials requires larger volumes and risks adsorption losses. [Decision] To achieve efficient digestion at nanoliter scale, chose to use SciProChip (Protein digestion vessel) to perform protein digestion, expecting to obtain peptides in ~78 nL volume.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Digestion of proteins into peptides at the nanoliter scale.

references.citation: S. T. Gebreyesus et al., Nat. Commun., 2022, 13, 37 (Ref 10)
references.relevance: Specifies the protein digestion vessel volume and function.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Peptides require desalting and cleanup prior to LC-MS injection. [Gap] External desalting steps (e.g., StageTips) involve transfer losses. [Decision] To achieve on-chip cleanup and avoid transfer loss, chose to use SciProChip (C18 desalting module) to perform peptide desalting, expecting to obtain cleaned peptides ready for analysis.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Efficient on-chip peptide clean-up obviating the need to externally perform peptide desalting.

references.citation: S. T. Gebreyesus et al., Nat. Commun., 2022, 13, 37 (Ref 10)
references.relevance: Describes the C18 beads-packed peptide desalting module.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Peptides need to be identified and quantified via mass spectrometry. [Gap] Data-dependent acquisition (DDA) suffers from missing values and stochastic nature compromising coverage. [Decision] To achieve enhanced and reproducible proteome identification, chose to use LC-MS/MS (DIA mode) to perform data-independent acquisition, expecting to obtain comprehensive peptide ion records.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 2.3-fold more protein identifications for mass limited samples when compared to DDA mode.

references.citation: S. T. Gebreyesus et al., Nat. Commun., 2022, 13, 37 (Ref 10)
references.relevance: Reports the introduction of DIA MS in this study and its performance.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Raw MS data requires processing to identify proteins. [Gap] Standard libraries may not optimize single-cell data completeness. [Decision] To maximize proteomic depth and reproducibility, chose to use Computational Pipeline (Spectral Library) to perform data analysis with sample-sized libraries, expecting to obtain ~1500 protein groups.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Average identification of ~1500 protein groups from a single mammalian cell.

references.citation: S. T. Gebreyesus et al., Nat. Commun., 2022, 13, 37 (Ref 10)
references.relevance: Confirms the proteome coverage achieved using this analysis approach.

proteome_depth.interpretation: High depth for single cell, meeting the goal of enhanced proteome depth.

sensitivity.unit: low abundant proteins (e.g., EGFR, TP53, CDK1)
sensitivity.interpretation: Capable of detecting druggable targets ranging from high abundance to low copy numbers.

sample_loss.interpretation: Streamlined workflow avoided sample losses related to sample transfers.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective to comprehensively characterize proteins at the single-cell level with enhanced depth and minimal loss was achieved using the microfluidics-based SciProChip-DIA workflow.
